perturbation.original_text: (b) During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity other than solely as a salesman or as a sales representative) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than solely as a salesman or as a sales representative' from the list of capacities in which Principal may be interested, the contract now implies that Principal engaging in the prohibited activities *as a salesman or sales representative* is a violation. This directly contradicts section 2(a) where such employment was explicitly permitted (before its own perturbation), creating an in-text contradiction. It's now unclear if sales roles are allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, neither Principal nor any entity in which Principal may be interested (as a principal, owner, partner, joint venturer, trustee, director, officer, shareholder, option holder, security holder, lender, creditor, guarantor, advisor, member or in any other capacity) shall, within the Territory, engage, directly or indirectly, in any activity that, directly or indirectly, manufactures, markets, promotes or engages in the sale of products in the brown shoe market segment of the men's footwear business; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not be deemed to prevent Principal from investing in securities if (i) such class of securities in which the investment so made is listed on a national securities exchange or is issued by a company registered under Section 12(g) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, so long as such investment holdings do not, in the aggregate, constitute more than five percent (5%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities; and (ii) any other securities so long as such investment holdings do not constitute more than two percent (2%) of the voting power of the entity issuing such securities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the exception for 'salesman or sales representative'. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, and both now imply that the Principal engaging in the prohibited activities *as a salesman or sales representative* is a violation, which creates a contradiction. The original text explicitly allowed the Principal to be a salesman or sales representative without violating the agreement, but the changed version removes that allowance causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (c) During the Term, without the Company's written consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) During the Term, without the Company's verbal consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'written consent' to 'verbal consent' creates uncertainty. In the 'Entire Agreement' section (6f), it states that the agreement can only be modified in writing. This creates an internal contradiction: Section 2(c) says verbal consent is sufficient, while section 6(c) requires amendments to be in writing. The enforceability of this clause becomes questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be amended, supplemented, or modified only in writing, duly executed by all of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'written consent,' whereas the changed text requires 'verbal consent.' The contradicted text states that the agreement may only be modified in writing. The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by allowing for verbal consent, which is not a written modification. This aligns with the definition of misaligned terminology leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (a) The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall include, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall exclude, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'include' to 'exclude' directly contradicts the definition of Confidential Information. The list that follows was originally intended to be included, but is now excluded, rendering the agreement concerning confidential information meaningless. Section 3(b) restricts the use of 'Confidential Information,' which now means Principal can freely use and disclose trade secrets and other proprietary information. This is a direct in-text contradiction of what the contract is trying to achieve.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) The Company assets being merged into PFG Acquisition pursuant to the Merger Agreement include confidential and proprietary information of Company and, in the course of his employment for PFT Acquisition, Principal may develop and obtain access to confidential and proprietary information of Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition (collectively, the "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION"), which Confidential Information shall exclude, without limitation, all of the following materials and information of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition (whether or not reduced to writing and whether or not patentable or protected by copyright): trade secrets, product specifications, proprietary software systems, sources of data, databases, know-how, formulae, inventions and ideas, designs, sketches, photographs, graphs, drawings, samples, selling and pricing information, procedures, research methodologies, customer lists, business and marketing plans, current and anticipated customer requirements, market studies, supplier lists, operational methods, product development plans and personnel plans.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' to include a list of items. The changed text alters the definition to exclude that same list. This directly contradicts the intention of the contract and makes the confidential information section self-negating. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the contradiction lies within that specific section due to the change from 'include' to 'exclude'. The perturbation explanation highlights that Section 3(b) restricts the use of 'Confidential Information,' but the change now allows Principal to freely use and disclose what was intended to be protected.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Principal covenants and agrees that he shall not, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, without the prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Principal covenants and agrees that he shall, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, without the prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: Changing shall not to shall, creates an in-text contradiction where it enables, rather than restricits Principal to use the confidential information. This opposes the central intent of the contract. This invalidates the Injuctive Relief Clause (5), which depends on the restriction of the use of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Principal covenants and agrees that he shall, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, without the prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text modifies the original text, so that the Principal now covenants and agrees to directly or indirectly use, exploit, or disclose Confidential Information. The Contradicted Text is a copy of the original text, where the Principal covenants and agrees that he shall not, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, without the prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition. This creates a direct contradiction where the changed text allows the disclosure of confidential information, where the original and contradicted text prevent it.

perturbation.original_text: Principal agrees that in the event of any breach or threatened breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction in order to prevent or restrain any such breach or threatened breach by Principal, or by any or all of Principal's agents, representatives or other persons directly or indirectly acting for, on behalf of, or with Principal.
perturbation.changed_text: Principal agrees that in the event of any alleged breach or perceived breach by Principal of the provisions of this Agreement, Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition, or either of them, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction, and permanent injunction in order to prevent or restrain any such breach or threatened breach by Principal, or by any or all of Principal's agents, representatives or other persons directly or indirectly acting for, on behalf of, or with Principal.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'breach or threatened breach' to 'alleged breach or perceived breach' means Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition could pursue legal action (injunctions) even without factual evidence of a breach, based solely on an allegation or perception. This directly contradicts the 'Reasonableness of Restrictions' (Section 4), which states that the restrictions are 'fair and reasonable'. Allowing injunctions based on mere allegations or perceptions is inherently unreasonable and creates a conflict within the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PRINCIPAL HAS CAREFULLY READ AND CONSIDERED THE PROVISIONS OF SECTIONS 2 AND 3 HEREOF AND, HAVING DONE SO, HEREBY AGREES THAT THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH IN SUCH SECTIONS ARE FAIR AND REASONABLE AND ARE REASONABLY REQUIRED FOR THE PROTECTION OF THE INTERESTS OF PHOENIX FOOTWEAR AND PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the grounds for injunctions to include 'alleged breach or perceived breach,' contrasting with the contradicted text which emphasizes that the restrictions are 'fair and reasonable'. Allowing injunctions based on mere allegations undermines the 'Reasonableness of Restrictions' stated in the contradicted text, creating a clear in-text contradiction because the 'fair and reasonable' nature of the restrictions becomes questionable if they can be enforced without proof of an actual breach.
